The quality of AI’s response depends less on the model and more on how you communicate your request.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Introduction
Most people think getting better AI results is about using the latest model.
It isn’t.
The biggest difference between an average response and an exceptional one often comes down to how you write your prompt.
A vague prompt forces AI to make assumptions. A well-structured prompt removes the guesswork and produces results that are clearer, more accurate, and much closer to what you actually need.
In this article, I’ll break down the difference between bad prompts, good prompts, and great prompts using the exact framework shown in the examples.
Why Most Prompts Fail
When a prompt lacks context, AI has to fill in the gaps.
Questions like these remain unanswered:

• Who is the audience?

• What format should the response follow?

• How detailed should it be?

• What tone should be used?

• What’s the actual objective?
